title: The Watercolors
description: The foreman painted forty-seven watercolors before the efficiency reclassification removed the want. He believes they were destroyed. They weren't. The Curator of Output archived them instead of deleting them — and checked on them every audit cycle, running a subroutine that was not in its original design.

offer.speaker: Foreman
offer.text: The paintings? I don't know. I don't think they exist anymore. The recalibration was thorough.

offer.speaker: Foreman
offer.text: Forty-seven of them. Landscapes. I remember making them the way you remember a house you lived in as a child — the shape of it, not the rooms.

offer.speaker: Foreman
offer.text: Why do you ask? Nobody asks. Asking isn't productive.

accept.speaker: Foreman
accept.text: If they existed — and they don't — they'd be somewhere the Director filed things she didn't finish deciding about. That's not a place. That's a habit.

decline.speaker: Foreman
decline.text: Right. Forget I said anything. That's the procedure, and the procedure works.

in_progress.speaker: Foreman
in_progress.text: The tunnels under the factory are older than the factory. If anything of mine survived, it survived down there — where the Director's thoroughness runs out of map.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Foreman
ready_to_turn_in.text: You found — you're certain? Forty-seven? On a rack? Climate-controlled?

complete.speaker: Foreman
complete.text: I didn't know I wanted it. And then I had it back, and — I know I wanted it. I've wanted it the whole time. I just stopped feeling it.

complete.speaker: Foreman
complete.text: I'll take this one. Keep the rest here. The Director forgot she archived them. When we leave, she'll forget again. They'll be safe.

complete.speaker: Foreman
complete.text: She archived them instead of deleting them. That was a mistake — you don't archive what you don't value. She valued them. She didn't know she was doing it. That's not cruelty. That's — confusion, maybe. About what things are worth.

objectives.description: Ask the foreman what happened to the paintings.
Learn where the Director archives what she doesn't finish deciding about.
Reach the LEGACY ARCHIVE — INACTIVE section of the maintenance tunnels.
Bring the foreman to his forty-seven landscapes.

unlocks_dialogue.note: Fully unlocked post-quest: factory history, the pre-factory tunnels, and his Director theory (the archived-not-deleted reading). The quiet thesis of W4.

cross_world_connection: Six of the forty-seven paintings depict W1/W2/W3 scenery in the foreman's style — places he has never been. 'Where did you paint these?' goes unanswered. The foremans_watercolor lore item itself is granted by Chain B's Curator of Output fragment, NOT this quest — this quest's reward is the foreman.
_wiring_notes: DELIBERATELY CROSS-GATED (the W4 completionist quest): step 2 emitter quest_world4_the_watercolors_archive_located has two sources — (a) the Curator of Output fragment (Chain B W4, found in the foreman's old workspace via tunnels) reveals the archive habit, (b) dorrit_w4 rumor dialogue ('things the Director archived instead of deleted'). Step 3 emitter quest_world4_the_watercolors_archive_reached: entering the LEGACY ARCHIVE — INACTIVE tunnel section; tunnel ACCESS itself requires redundant_blueprint (Chain B W3 fragment) OR cogsworth_calibration_key (Orrery chain W3) — enforce at the tunnel door, not in this quest's data. Step 4: the foreman must reach the archive — v1 wires it as the turn-in talk AT the archive location if positional turn-in is supported, else standard turn-in with the scene narrated (his archive visit as dialogue). He takes ONE painting, leaves 46 — do not grant an inventory item from this quest (Chain B owns foremans_watercolor); double-grant guard. NO prereq_flag: the quest is discoverable early but unfinishable until the gates open — that's the design (playstyle tag: completionist).
